questions.q: Who founded the Arya Samaj?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj was founded by Swami Dayananda Saraswati.

questions.q: In which year was the Arya Samaj founded?
questions.explanation: Swami Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj called for a return to which ancient scriptures?
questions.explanation: Its central slogan was 'Back to the Vedas,' urging a return to Vedic teachings.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj campaigned against which social evil?
questions.explanation: It opposed social ills such as caste rigidity and untouchability.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj placed strong emphasis on what, contributing to national consciousness?
questions.explanation: Its emphasis on education and self-respect helped raise national consciousness.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj's founder is honoured with which title?
questions.explanation: Dayananda Saraswati was a Swami, a Hindu monastic title.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the Arya Samaj first established in 1875?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj was first established in Bombay in 1875.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj rejected which common religious practice?
questions.explanation: It rejected idol worship, advocating worship based on the Vedas instead.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj was a movement primarily of which religion's reform?
questions.explanation: It was a movement for the reform of Hinduism along Vedic lines.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj is regarded as part of which broad nineteenth-century trend?
questions.explanation: It was part of the nineteenth-century socio-religious reform movements in India.

questions.q: Swami Dayananda Saraswati's principal book setting out his ideas was titled what?
questions.explanation: His major work was 'Satyartha Prakash' (The Light of Truth), the chief text of the Arya Samaj.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj promoted a purification ceremony to bring people into the Vedic fold called what?
questions.explanation: The Shuddhi movement aimed to reconvert or purify people into the Vedic-Hindu fold.

questions.q: Which network of schools and colleges grew from the Arya Samaj's emphasis on education?
questions.explanation: The Dayanand Anglo-Vedic (DAV) schools and colleges spread from the Arya Samaj's educational mission.

questions.q: Swami Dayananda Saraswati was born in 1824 in which region of India?
questions.explanation: He was born in 1824 at Tankara in Gujarat.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj became especially strong and influential in which region of India?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj grew particularly strong in Punjab and northern India.

questions.q: Which freedom fighter known as the 'Lion of Punjab' was strongly associated with the Arya Samaj?
questions.explanation: Lala Lajpat Rai was a prominent leader closely linked to the Arya Samaj movement.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj advocated which practice for widows, opposing their lifelong restriction?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj actively promoted widow remarriage and opposed child marriage.

questions.q: Swami Dayananda Saraswati's guru, who taught him Vedic grammar, was who?
questions.explanation: His teacher was the blind scholar Swami Virajanand of Mathura.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj opposed which idea by insisting that caste should be based on merit rather than birth?
questions.explanation: It rejected hereditary caste, arguing that varna should rest on qualities and deeds, not birth.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj's fire ritual, central to its worship in place of idol worship, is called what?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj uses the Vedic fire ritual, havan or yajna, in worship instead of idols.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj's emphasis on Hindi and the Devanagari script supported the growth of what?
questions.explanation: Its promotion of Hindi and Devanagari contributed to a sense of national-language identity.

questions.q: The Arya Samaj is often compared as a more assertive counterpart to which earlier Bengal reform body?
questions.explanation: It is often contrasted with the earlier Brahmo Samaj of Bengal as a more assertive Vedic reform movement.

questions.q: In which year did Swami Dayananda Saraswati, founder of the Arya Samaj, die?
questions.explanation: Swami Dayananda Saraswati died in 1883, reportedly at Ajmer.

questions.q: The first 'Satyartha Prakash' by Dayananda was published in which year?
questions.explanation: The first edition of 'Satyartha Prakash' was published in 1875, the same year the Samaj was founded.

questions.q: After Dayananda's death the Arya Samaj split into two factions over diet and education; one faction was led by which DAV-linked moderate?
questions.explanation: The 'College' (moderate) faction was associated with Lala Hans Raj and the DAV movement, while the 'Gurukul' faction followed Swami Shraddhanand.

questions.q: The Gurukul faction of the Arya Samaj founded the famous Gurukul Kangri near which town?
questions.explanation: Swami Shraddhanand founded the Gurukul Kangri near Haridwar in 1902.

questions.q: Swami Dayananda formulated how many core principles, or niyams, of the Arya Samaj?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj is guided by ten principles (niyams) formulated by Dayananda.

questions.q: The first DAV (Dayanand Anglo-Vedic) school opened in 1886 in which city?
questions.explanation: The first DAV school was established in Lahore in 1886.

questions.q: Dayananda's monotheistic worship of one God invoked which sacred Vedic syllable as the primary name of God?
questions.explanation: The Arya Samaj holds 'Om' (Aum) as the foremost name of the one formless God.

questions.q: Swami Shraddhanand, an Arya Samaj leader who joined the national movement, was assassinated in which year?
questions.explanation: Swami Shraddhanand, a leading Arya Samajist and nationalist, was assassinated in 1926.
